description.pass1_value: Clay is a no-code / low-code workspace for building and automating data tables and workflows, with webhooks and a REST API to programmatically read and write rows in tables.
description.final_value: Clay is an AI-powered platform for scaling personalized outbound and automating GTM workflows, using a table-based workflow builder to enrich, score, and reach out to leads.

pass1_value.note: Clay uses API keys presented as bearer tokens in the Authorization header (Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>) per the API Keys help article. The Webhooks & API article also shows Authorization: Bearer usage for REST endpoints.

final_value.note: Clay’s Developers page instructs to create an API key in Clay and authenticate to https://api.clay.com/v1/ using the Authorization header in the form `Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_API_KEY>`.

pass1_value.note: The Webhooks and API help article documents a REST API under https://api.clay.com/v1/ with CRUD endpoints for tables and rows (GET /v1/tables, POST /v1/tables/{id}/rows, PATCH /v1/tables/{id}/rows/{row_id}, etc.), indicating broad coverage of core product objects (tables, rows).

final_value.note: Clay’s Developers page documents a REST API with base URL `https://api.clay.com/v1/` and lists endpoints for core objects including tables and rows (e.g., `GET /v1/tables`, `POST /v1/tables/{table_id}/rows`, `PATCH /v1/tables/{table_id}/rows/{row_id}`, `DELETE /v1/tables/{table_id}/rows/{row_id}`).

pass1_value.note: Public REST API docs and self-serve API key generation exist in the help center; a developer can sign up and obtain keys to start integrating immediately.

final_value.note: Clay’s public Developers page documents how to create an API key (Settings → API keys), the API base URL, and example REST endpoints; a developer can sign up and begin integrating immediately.
